english: porter Cs. — rkaṅ-grós (rkang gros) or -brós (-bros) 1. walk-ing on foot. 2. domestic cattle; breeding-cattle. — rkaṅ-rgyú (rkang rgyu) Cs.: 'the hollow of the sole'. — rkaṅ-yčíg-pa (rkang gcig pa) one-footed. — rkaṅ-rjén (rkang rjen) bare-footed. — rkaṅ-rjés (rkang rjes) footstep, trace. — rkaṅ-gnyís-pa (rkang gnyis pa) two-footed, a biped, po. for man, mankind. — rkaṅ-stégs (rkang stegs) foot-stool; trestle. — rkaṅ-tháṅ (rkang thang) on foot, rkaṅ-tháṅ-pa (rkang thang pa) pedestrian. foot-soldier, rkaṅ-tháṅ-du (rkang thang du) grúl-ba (grul ba) (Sch. also: rkaṅ-tháṅ-ba (rkang thang ba)) to walk, to go on foot. — rkaṅ-mthíl (rkang mthil) sole of the foot. — rkaṅ-thúṅ (rkang thung) (erron. also -thuṅ (-thung)) Ssk. पादप 'drinking with the foot' po. for: tree Mil. — rkaṅ-dúṅ (rkang dung) trumpet made of a human thigh-bone (Hook. I 173). — rkaṅ-drúg-pa (rkang drug pa), rkaṅ-drúg-ldan-pa (rkang drug ldan pa) six-footed; insect, po. — rkaṅ-gdúb (rkang gdub) foot-ring (ornament). — rkaṅ-gdréṅ (rkang gdren) (v. also zabs-gdréṅ (zhabs gdren)) disgrace, rkaṅ gdréṅ-pa (rkang gdren pa) c. genit. to get a person into disgrace, to deprive him of his honour and good name, to be a disgrace to another, e. g. a son to his father by criminal actions etc. rkaṅ-rdúm (rkang rdum) a maimed foot; lame Cs. — rkaṅ-snám (rkang snam) trowsers, snam-bui (snam bu'i) rkaṅ-snám (rkang snam) yčíg (gcig) one pair of cloth-trowsers. rkaṅ pags lhuṅ (rkang pags lhung) S. g. fol. 9? — rkaṅ-phyéṅ (rkang phyeng) felt for covering the legs, v. rkaṅ-dkrí (rkang dkri). — rkaṅ-ból (rkang bol) upper part of the foot. — rkaṅ bam (rkang bam) a disease in the foot, Sch.: gout. rkaṅ-brós (rkang bros) or brós (bros) v. rkaṅ-grós (rkang gros). — rkaṅ-tségs (rkang tshigs) v. tségs (tshigs). — rkaṅ-mdzúb-dzér-pa (rkang mdzub dzer pa) Sch.: corn (on the toe). — rkaṅ-mdzér (rkang mdzer) iron pricks fastened to the feet for climb-ing mountains. — rkaṅ-bźí-pa (rkang bzhi pa) four-footed; quadruped. — rkaṅ-lág (rkang lag) hands and feet, rkaṅ-lag (rkang lag) bsál-ba (bsal ba) Lt., Schr.: 'numbness or rheumatic pain in hands and feet'; rkaṅ-lág (rkang lag) sér-kar (ser kar) śoṅ (shong) hands and feet chap Sch. — rkaṅ-lám (rkang lam) foot-path. rkaṅ-śíṅ (rkang shing) treadle, of a loom. — rkaṅ-śúbs (rkang shubs) stocking, sock. — rkaṅ sór (rkang sor) toe.
I. vb. to desire, to long, nór-la (nor la) for money. II. sbst. 1. longing (cf. kám (skam) extr.) — 2. v. skám-pa (skam pa).
pf. (b)rkús ((b)rkus), ft. brkú (brku), imp. rkús (rkus), to steal, to rob, brkú-bya (brku bya) to be stolen, brkú-byai rdzas (brku bya'i rdzas) things that may be stolen.

1. thief frq. 2. theft. rk. byéd-pa (rk. byed pa) (W.: *čo-čè* (*cho che*)) to steal; *kún-ma zos soṅ* (*kun ma zos song*) W. it has been carried away by a thief; *ka-kún gyáb-čè* (*ka kun rgyab che*) W. 'to steal with the mouth', to promise to pay without ever doing so, or: to deny having known a thing missing, until all inquiry has ceased and it may be safely appro-priated (a common practice of servants in India); dur-rkún (dur rkun) robber of graves. — rkún-tabs-su (rkun thabs su) bláṅ-ba (blang ba) to take away thievishly Stg. — rkun-nór (rkun nor) stolen goods. — rkún-po (rkun po), fem. -mo (-mo) thief. — rkún-dpon (rkun dpon) the head of a gang of thieves or robbers Cs. — rkún-zla (rkun zla) a thief's accomplice.
(Lex.) 1. the anus B. — 2. vulva W., C. — 3. backside, poste-riors C. — rkub-stégs (rkub stegs) sitting-bench C. — rkub-tsós (rkub tshos) buttocks Cs.

also skéd-pa (sked pa), W.: *skéd-pa* (*sked pa*) 1. the waist, séṅ-ges mčóṅs-sar (seng ges mchongs sar) was mčóṅs (mchongs) rkéd-pa čág (rked pa chag) if the fox takes a lion's leap, he breaks his neck Mil.; *skyéd kúg táṅ-čè* (*skyed kug stang che*) W. to bow; *skéd-zér* (*sked zer*) (?) the arms a-kimbo W.; more particularly that part where the girdle is worn, loins; rkéd-rgyan (rked rgyan) ornament of the girdle; rkéd-pa-nas (rked pa nas) grí (gri) btón (bton) he took a knife from his girdle Pth.; *kè-pa bháb* (*khe pa bhab*) 'her waist fell', euphem. expression for: she has got her menses C. — 2. the middle of a building, of a mountain, *kar-skyéd* (*mkhar skyed*) W. the mid-dle story of a castle; rkéd-pa tsam (rked pa tsam) brtsígs tsár nas (brtsigs tshar nas) when the building was half finished Glr.; Ti-sei (ti se'i) rkéd-pa-na (rked pa na) yár bslébs soṅ (yar bslebs song) he is already half-way up the Ti-se Mil.

pf. (b)rkós ((b)rkos), ft. brkó (brko), imp. rkós (rkos) 1. to dig, dig-out, to hoe, e. g. sa (sa) earth; rko-byéd (rko byed) digger; po. also a spade, mattock; brkó.byai sa (brko bya'i sa) soil to be turned up. — 2. to engrave (cf. búr-ba ('bur ba)); brko-spyád (brko spyad) a gouge Sch.; brkós ma (brkos ma) sculpture.
n. of a bird Wdn., prob. = ko-ma (ko ma).
